content:
> @cpboyd any chance you make a PR here ?

I believe that he's abandoned his work towards adding his code to the public plugin. I've taken it over [here](https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/4638)
@PiotrMitkowski Hope your PR will be merged soon, this issue has been going on for too long.
Any updates?
@idrats Looks like it will be fixed soon. [here](https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/5603)
Hi everyone, any updates? I have upgrade image_picker to the latest version and still have issue on IOS. Maybe someone has tried

Hi @AlehSabadashGmail, I'm still working on a group of PRs for this change. Please follow https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/4638 for progress, as this PR will have the actual change for the `image_picker` package and will be landed as the last one. Cheers!
@PiotrMitkowski Thank you very much for your answer, last question, when are going to release it ?

First out of 3 PRs is merged (platform interface), there're two more to go. It should be achievable to complete them by the end of June. I'll post a comment when it's done.

Note that I'm not working on fixing the exact issue with "Select Photos" permission - my change will allow picking images without asking for permission (unless you need some specific image metadata).
Once all the PR are merged will this plugin finally work correctly with "Select Photos" permission?   Is anyone actively working on this issue?
Any update with this? I still got that weird error.
Still showing all images and videos when clicked select photo. Is it OK when opened in appmarket?
Two years passed， still showing all photos , And, if the Apple audit found this, would it really be OK?
Still getting same issue please address it
any updates on this ?
waiting for @cyanglaz review for https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/5915
The fix that was made is not complete. It solves the problem with the photo but does not solve the problem with the video :(

## [profiler] Mach thread_info kernel call fails while trying to mach_msg_send. Error MACH_SEND_INVALID_DEST

<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

Since upgrading to **Flutter 1.20.0**, when I `flutter run` my app using the iOS Simulator, I sometimes see 1 or more of this warning:
```
An Observatory debugger and profiler on iPhone 11 Pro Max is available at: http://127.0.0.1:49425/MFzU_2A99ig=/
[VERBOSE-2:profiler_metrics_ios.mm(184)] Error retrieving thread information: (ipc/send) invalid destination port
[VERBOSE-2:profiler_metrics_ios.mm(184)] Error retrieving thread information: (ipc/send) invalid destination port
```

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

I expect no errors.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

<!-- Finally, paste the output of running `flutter doctor -v` here. -->

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 1.20.0, on Mac OS X 10.15.6 19G73, locale en-US)

    • Framework revision 840c9205b3 (19 hours ago), 2020-08-04 20:55:12 -0700

    • Dart version 2.9.0
```

hI @awhitford 
not able to reproduce, i have run the flutter multiples on `stable` channel all day
Can you please reproduce it again? please provide your `flutter run --verbose` and a complete reproducible minimal code sample
Thank you

**The issue is show the flutter page is blank.**
i have the same issue. How can I fix it?

I have the same issue this morning I made the flutter upgrade, now the application is running perfectly all synchrone and async functions work perfect but I have this message on terminal : "[VERBOSE-2:profiler_metrics_ios.mm(184)] Error retrieving thread information: (ipc/send) invalid destination port"

Oh sorry I apology for my msg, just after writing my previous comments I remember that after the upgrade I dosent restart my computer.
I restart it and now the error msg disappear
I have also the same issue. I tried restarting my computer, the app and rebugging it again, but it didn't disappear. Does anyone has ANY SOLUTION please? Thanks!
have the same issue.

> Oh sorry I apology for my msg, just after writing my previous comments I remember that after the upgrade I dosent restart my computer.
> I restart it and now the error msg disappear

I have the same issue. But Restarting the System didn't do the trick. Still wonder what error it is?
Same issue.
I have the same issue.
Same, have it since before I upgraded to 1.20.0 when I switched to the beta channel a few weeks ago.
I have the same issue.
I have the same issue since I upgraded to 1.20.0.

On Channel stable, 1.20.1. Same issue 👎 
[VERBOSE-2:profiler_metrics_ios.mm(184)] Error retrieving thread information: (ipc/send) invalid destination port

same issue! 

[VERBOSE-2:profiler_metrics_ios.mm(184)] Error retrieving thread information: (ipc/send) invalid destination port .

I had this issue as well, restarting the simulator solved it for me. Restarting my Mac didn't help, because by default it restores all windows including the simulator, which might still be a badly cached version or something.

> I had this issue as well, restarting the simulator solved it for me. Restarting my Mac didn't help, because by default it restores all windows including the simulator, which might still be a badly cached version or something.

Fixed it temporarily then came back not sure if the same happened to you.
I have the same issue since I upgraded to 1.20.0.
Same issue here since upgrading to 1.20 and 1.20.1: However, the app seems to run just fine.
> Same issue here since upgrading to 1.20 and 1.20.1: > > > > However, the app seems to run just fine. same issue as well. The apps runs fine tho
restarting simulator worked for me too

> **The issue is show the flutter page is blank.**

I have solved this problem, check the pubspec.yaml,there are blank dir by the  assets.

This should not happen in any of the release modes and should not affect rendering or execution in other modes. It seems like the call to [task_threads](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/41971f48c370a6a3e29ff623b2d46008a9a83bf5/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/profiler_metrics_ios.mm#L161) is failing. @iskakaushik for thoughts on what to do in case of this failure? Maybe disable the profiling?
same problem (flutter 1.20.2 on iOS), I have downgraded to 1.17.5 to solve it
I started having this issue when I upgraded from 1.17 to 1.20. My app gets stuck on the splash screen. It happens on an iOS simulator and a physical device.

I downgraded to v1.17.5 and I still get the error:
`[VERBOSE-2:profiler_metrics_ios.mm(184)] Error retrieving thread information: (ipc/send) invalid destination port`
+1, Flutter 1.20.1 on iOS
Same

@Development4A it shouldn't be related to this error. My guess would be that you're pulling the data that populates the list asynchronously (like from a db) and not awaiting it or setting the state afterwards. However, I would need more information to confirm it. Since this is probably unrelated to this issue I'd suggest opening a new one where you specify more information.
is there any fix for this issue yet? I'm experiencing the same..

i was able to fix the problem

1. I downgraded to v1.17.5 from v1.20
`flutter version v1.17.5`
2. rename folder "ios" to "ios1"
3. next
`flutter create -i swift .`
in result i have new folder "ios" with project
4. `flutter build ios` for install pods
5. set up the ios project again and copied necessary files for me from "ios1"
6. `flutter run` without any problem
I am still having this problem. I don't wanna downgrade my flutter version. 😔
